## [00:00 - 02:22]

This is the Pubcast Shot. Yeah. I'm John Loomer. Let's go. So one of the dilemmas that Facebook advertisers face is related to placements. Which placements should you use? Or should you use automatic placements or manual placements? So with automatic placements, and that's what Facebook recommends by the way, Facebook will maximize your budget, according to Facebook, using a combination of placements that will get you the most results for the lowest cost. If you choose to do it manually, you think you can do better, you choose which placements are best, and which to ignore. So there are though potential issues with each approach. If you use automatic placements, Facebook may not distribute budget to the placements properly. That's entirely possible, especially if you're optimizing for something like engagement or clicks. Facebook could get you a whole bunch of accidental clicks or low quality clicks that don't mean anything, but as far as Facebook knows, they did their job. And that may be more likely to happen in a placement like audience network, for example. But if you go manually, you may remove potentially effective placements without knowing it. Or you may go only with newsfeed as an example, which may be the most expensive. You may overthink it. Now understand that every placement is different. Different copy and creative requirements, different potential quality of engagement, different costs. A good tip I have for you is to use breakdowns within ads manager. So you click that breakdown drop down menu and select by delivery and then placement. You can then see the cost of each action by placement. You may be surprised. Do me a favor. Did you listen to the pubcast shot? Did you like it? Let me know. Tweet me on Twitter at John Loomer. Find me on Facebook, facebook.com, John Loomer Digital. Drop me a message info at JohnLoomer.com. It really helps if I get that feedback. Thanks for joining me. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
